title: crotalaria stipularia var. prostrata
intro: Crotalaria stipularia var. prostrata, also known as the creeping rattlebox or creeping crotalaria, is a perennial plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its prostrate growth habit and clusters of yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Crotalaria stipularia var. prostrata include treating fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as providing relief from respiratory problems and wounds. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
